tasks.description: KeyError from case-sensitive email lookup. The lookup_user function uses the raw email as dict key without normalizing case, causing KeyError when email has different casing.
String-typed price comparison ordering wrong. Prices loaded from CSV remain as strings, causing lexicographic comparison where '9.99' > '10.00' evaluates to True.
asyncio deadlock from nested event loop. batch_fetch calls asyncio.run() inside an already-running event loop, causing RuntimeError about nested event loops.
Circular import causing AttributeError at runtime. user.py imports from auth.py which imports from user.py, creating a circular dependency that fails when accessing attributes.
Catastrophic regex backtracking in input validation. The regex pattern ^(a+)+$ causes exponential backtracking on adversarial input, making validation hang indefinitely.
Nullish coalescing mishandles falsy 0/empty-string. Using ?? instead of || means 0 and empty string are treated as valid values when they should fall through to defaults.
Unhandled promise rejection in error path. The error handler returns without awaiting the cleanup promise, leaving database connections open on failure.
Type narrowing lost across async boundary. A type guard narrows a union type, but after an await the narrowing is lost and TypeScript widens the type back.
ESM/CJS interop causing runtime import failure. An ESM-only package is imported using CJS require syntax; works in dev with transpilation but fails in production build.
EventEmitter listener leak across hot-reload cycles. Listeners registered in useEffect without cleanup accumulate on HMR, causing memory leak and duplicate event handling.
DB connection leak on coroutine cancellation. The finally block performs a suspending cleanup but without NonCancellable context, so cleanup is skipped when the coroutine is cancelled.
Non-exhaustive when on sealed class. A when expression over a sealed class is missing a branch for a recently added subclass, causing a runtime NoWhenBranchMatchedException.
Platform type from Java interop causing NPE. A Java method returns null but Kotlin code receives it as a non-nullable platform type, causing NullPointerException on access.
Flow collection on wrong dispatcher blocking UI. A Flow is collected on Dispatchers.Main but the collector performs blocking IO, freezing the UI thread.
Blocking call inside suspend function stalling thread pool. Thread.sleep() is called inside a suspend fun instead of delay(), blocking the entire coroutine dispatcher and starving other coroutines.
Goroutine leak from unbuffered channel with no reader. A goroutine writes to an unbuffered channel but the reader was removed, causing the goroutine to block forever and leak.
Slice append mutating shared backing array. Two slices created from the same source share a backing array; append to one silently corrupts the other.
Nil interface vs nil concrete type comparison. A function returns (*ConcreteType)(nil) as an error interface; the caller checks err != nil and gets true because the interface wraps a typed nil.
Context cancellation not propagated to child goroutines. Parent context is cancelled but child goroutine uses context.Background() instead of the derived context, ignoring cancellation.
Concurrent map write causing runtime panic. Multiple goroutines write to a map[string]int without synchronization, triggering the Go runtime's concurrent map write detection.
Dangling reference from returned local borrow. A function creates a local String and tries to return a reference to it, but the borrow outlives the owner.
Use after move in error handling path. A value is moved into the Ok branch of a match, then referenced in the Err branch for the error message, causing a use-after-move error.
Unwrap panic on None from HashMap::get in multi-threaded context. HashMap::get().unwrap() panics when a key is absent due to a race condition in concurrent access.
Type not implementing Send used across thread boundary. Rc<RefCell<T>> is used across a thread spawn boundary but Rc is not Send, causing a compiler error.
Self-referential future unpinned causing undefined behavior. A self-referential struct is used as a future without Pin, and moving the future after polling causes UB.
